extracted.brandBackground: Amazing Athletes offers developmental sports education and physical fitness programs to children aged 18 months to 12 years.

leadership.name: Adam Geisler
leadership.background: Has served as CEO since October 2018 and also leads other youth sports brands under Super Sports Holdings.
leadership.whyItMatters: Provides unified executive leadership across multiple youth sports franchise systems.

leadership.name: John Erlandson
leadership.background: President since October 2018 with over 17 years of business development experience in sporting goods and licensing.
leadership.whyItMatters: Directs the brand's strategic growth and day-to-day franchise operations.

leadership.name: Jared Taylor
leadership.background: Appointed President in January 2024 after serving as Director of Franchise Development and owning franchises since 2014.
leadership.whyItMatters: Brings direct franchisee experience and system-specific coaching expertise to the leadership team.

leadership.name: Khalil A. Haddad
leadership.background: CFO since October 2018 and serves as CFO for multiple affiliated youth sports brands.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees financial management and shared services across the parent portfolio.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: Disputes must be resolved by arbitration or litigation only in New York.

operationalRisks.title: Mandatory Minimum Payments
operationalRisks.description: Minimum royalty and advertising fund payments are required regardless of sales volume.

operationalRisks.title: Spousal Liability
operationalRisks.description: Spouses must sign a personal guarantee making them liable for all financial obligations.

riskReasons: 2 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.

notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.

underwriting.assessment: $250,000 liquid covers the $87,550 mid-point build-out without financing, though that leaves little working-capital cushion. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $20,498/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: The franchise provides developmental sports education and physical fitness programs to children.

benchmark.label: Education / childcare
benchmark.dominantRisk: Regulated staff ratios and enrollment/utilization. Labor dominates and ratios cap how lean you can run.
considerations: Labor is the dominant line at ~40–55% and is floored by regulated student/staff ratios.
Validate enrollment ramp and capacity-utilization curves with Item 20 operators.
Confirm licensing, facility, and compliance costs specific to your state — not in the FDD.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–20% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

insights.staffingNote: Flagged as an lightly staffed model from the FDD, so labor is adjusted down to 20–33% — the 40–55% category band assumes a staffed venue. Confirm actual staffing with Item 20 franchisees.

buildup.label: − Occupancy / rent (not disclosed)
buildup.note: Item 7 disclosed no rent figure — benchmark occupancy applied so the line above isn't overstated

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: lightly staffed · ≈ 1.2–2 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Education / childcare” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Read as lightly staffed from the FDD, which lowers the labor band applied below.
Item 7 disclosed no rent — occupancy estimated at the 10–18% category band.
Estimated at the 5–12% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 20–33% lightly staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor's statements don't show the distress signals we screen for — positive net worth and a clean audit. Still worth a quick confirmation against current figures.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. There is no parent guarantee of performance in this FDD, so Amazing Athletes, LLC is not contractually obligated to keep funding the franchisor or backing its commitments to you.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: no parent guarantee backing the franchisor's obligations to you
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Derived from the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
